hero.title: 3 Days in Bangalore.
hero.titleAccent: Gardens, Palaces and India's Coolest City.
hero.intro: Bangalore wears many hats. Garden city, tech capital, craft-beer pioneer, and home to some genuinely beautiful heritage. Three easy days to sample all of it, at a pace that suits you.

breadcrumb.label: 3 Days Bangalore Highlights: Culture, Gardens & More

overview.heading: 3 Days Bangalore Highlights: Culture, Gardens & More: The Full Picture
body: Bangalore, now officially Bengaluru, is the easiest big Indian city to love. The climate is mild all year thanks to its high elevation, the parks are genuinely green, and the food scene runs from old-school dosa joints to some of India's best modern restaurants. It is a city that rewards a relaxed pace.

This 3 day Bangalore tour package from Malaysia is private throughout, with your own vehicle and driver guide for full flexibility. Visit Lalbagh Botanical Garden, the grand Bangalore Palace, Tipu Sultan's summer retreat, and the buzzing shopping and dining of MG Road and Brigade Road on your own schedule.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Lalbagh and Cubbon Park
items.body: Bangalore earned its garden city name honestly. Lalbagh's 240-year-old botanical garden and the leafy expanse of Cubbon Park are perfect early-morning escapes before the city wakes up.

items.title: Bangalore Palace & Tipu Sultan's Fort
items.body: The Tudor-style Bangalore Palace and the elegant teak summer palace of Tipu Sultan show two very different sides of the city's royal past. Both are worth the visit for the architecture alone.

items.title: Food, Cafes & Craft Beer
items.body: From legendary masala dosa at decades-old eateries to leafy cafes and India's original craft-beer scene, Bangalore eats and drinks very well. Your guide knows where the locals go.

days.title: Arrive Bangalore
days.description: Met at Kempegowda International Airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening free to settle in around MG Road or Indiranagar.
suggestedActivities: Settle in around MG Road or Indiranagar

days.title: Full Day Bangalore Sightseeing
days.description: Your private vehicle and guide are with you all day for a relaxed loop of the city's gardens, palaces and markets.

days.title: Free Morning, Depart
days.description: Checkout at noon. A final cafe breakfast or some shopping before your airport transfer.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: South India Specialists Since 1988
whyBook.body: Bangalore is an easy city, but a good local driver guide turns a decent trip into a smooth one, threading the traffic and getting you to the gardens before the crowds. Dhesu has run South India tours for over 35 years.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Bangalore Tour Package from Malaysia: A Relaxed City Break
sections.body:
Bangalore is one of the most comfortable Indian cities for a first short trip. The weather is mild year-round, the people are easygoing, and the mix of gardens, heritage and food gives you plenty without any rush.

This private package puts a vehicle and driver guide at your disposal, so you skip the traffic stress and cover Lalbagh, the palaces and the shopping streets at your own pace.

Bangalore also makes a natural base for Mysore and Ooty, which is why our 5 day Bangalore, Mysore and Ooty package is so popular as a follow-on.

sections.heading: Is Three Days in Bangalore Enough?
sections.body:
For the city itself, yes. Three days covers the gardens, the palaces, the markets and the food comfortably.

If you want hill stations and palaces too, extend into the 5 day Bangalore, Mysore and Ooty scenic toy train route, or the 7 day Discover South India circuit.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Kempegowda International Airport, Bangalore (BLR). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Bangalore?
items.answer: Bangalore is pleasant almost year-round thanks to its elevation. September to February is the most comfortable, with mild days and cool evenings. The monsoon (June to September) brings refreshing rain but rarely disrupts sightseeing.

items.question: Is Bangalore good for first-time India travellers?
items.answer: Very. The mild climate, cosmopolitan feel, wide use of English and relaxed pace make it one of the gentlest introductions to India for Malaysians.

items.question: Can I extend to Mysore and Ooty?
items.answer: Yes. Mysore is about 3 hours away and Ooty a scenic hill drive beyond. Our 5 day Bangalore, Mysore and Ooty package combines all three with the famous Nilgiri toy train.
